_meta.title: Diamonds in the Dust — Quantitative Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Saurabh Mukherjea, Rakshit Ranjan, Salil Desai (Marcellus, 2021)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for fw_diamond in scoring_engine.py and diamonds_pass output column. Proxy design where CSV lacks 10Y cumulative series or 3YB receivable history.
_meta.design_rationale: Mukherjea's Three-Lens framework is the forensic layer above Coffee Can and Unusual Billionaires. Coffee Can gates entry (10Y ROCE >= 15%, 10Y sales CAGR >= 10%). Diamonds adds Gate Zero (governance integrity), Lens 1 (cash earnings quality), Lens 2 (moat durability), Lens 3 (capital allocation self-sufficiency). The framework is additive, not a replacement. Key distinction: CFO/PAT threshold is 80% (not Coffee Can's EBITDA variant), D/E < 0.5 (strictest across all Mukherjea books), and FCF/CFO >= 25% is the unique Lens 3 signal absent from all prior frameworks.
_meta.implementation_note: Two gates require multi-year cumulative data not available in StockScan CSV: (1) year-by-year CFO/PAT check, (2) 10Y cumulative FCF/CFO series. Best-effort proxies are used and documented in known_gaps. The implemented gate set covers 9 of the 11 canonical Diamond gates.

gate_zero_integrity._purpose: Binary prerequisite layer. Any single breach = immediate exclusion before quantitative sorting.

promoter_holding_floor.rationale: Mukherjea Chapter 2: promoter must hold majority enough to make long-duration capital allocation decisions without institutional pressure. 40% is the specific threshold cited — lower than Mayer's 50% because Mukherjea focuses on established compounders where institutional quality is already proven.

pledge_ceiling.rationale: Mukherjea Chapter 2: promoter pledge > 10% indicates financial distress — the promoter has used equity as debt collateral. A margin call on pledged shares forces distress selling regardless of business fundamentals, creating binary tail risk. fillna(100) to conservatively fail stocks with unknown pledge data.
pledge_ceiling.fillna_strategy: fillna(100) — unknown pledge data fails conservatively

lens_1_clean_accounts._purpose: Verifies reported accounting earnings are backed by real operating cash flows. Eliminates window-dressed P&Ls.

cfo_to_pat_floor.rationale: Mukherjea Chapter 3: CFO/PAT < 0.8 for any individual year = earnings are not cash-backed. The ideal check is year-by-year compliance (not a smoothed average). The CSV provides only the trailing 12M figure — this is therefore a point-in-time proxy for the annual check. 80% threshold is derived from the book's 0.8 ratio. Note: Dorsey also uses 80%. Coffee Can uses CFO/EBITDA (different denominator). This is the strictest cash quality gate in the platform after Dorsey.
cfo_to_pat_floor.unit_note: cfo_to_pat is PERCENTAGE in CSV (80.4 = 80.4%). fillna(0) fails null conservatively.
cfo_to_pat_floor.known_limitation: Year-by-year compliance requires 10Y annual CFO/PAT series — not in CSV. Point-in-time proxy misses companies that manipulate earnings only in off-years.

dso_rolling_3y_max_rise.rationale: Mukherjea Chapter 3: if DSO rises > 15 days over any 3-year window, management is stuffing the channel or granting loose credit to manufacture revenue. dso_delta_3y = days_receivable - days_receivable_3yb (TRUE 3-year delta — Days Receivable 3 Years Back added to CSV). fillna(999) to conservatively fail stocks with missing receivable history.

forensic_gate.rationale: Diamond-specific forensic subset: only the 6 flags directly mapped to Mukherjea's Three-Lens Framework. The generic red_flag_count (27 flags) includes standards from Vijay Malik, Coffee Can, WCS24, Ind AS 116, and sector-specific signals that the Diamonds book never checks. Applying them to Diamond would penalise companies for failing frameworks they were not designed for. dm_forensic_flag_count == 0 means all six Diamond-relevant forensic checks pass cleanly.
dm_flag_subset: rf_low_cfo_pat — Lens 1: CFO/PAT < 70% (cash does not back earnings)
rf_high_accruals — Lens 1: Beneish TATA > 5% of assets (strongest single fraud predictor)
rf_high_receivables — Lens 1: absolute DSO above sector threshold (channel stuffing)
rf_receivables_bloat — Lens 1: DSO expanding faster than sector peers (systematic manipulation)
rf_rising_debt — Gate Zero: D/E trajectory rising (balance sheet deteriorating even if level < 0.5)
rf_dilution — Gate Zero: share dilution >= 3% (promoter misalignment via equity erosion)

excluded_flags_rationale.rf_low_cfo_ebitda: Coffee Can Investing standard (CFO/EBITDA); Diamonds uses CFO/PAT — different denominator, different book
excluded_flags_rationale.rf_pledge_elevated: Redundant — pledge < 10 is already a direct hard gate in fw_diamond
excluded_flags_rationale.rf_negative_fcf: Redundant — FCF/CFO >= 25% direct gate already excludes negative FCF
excluded_flags_rationale.rf_fcf_to_cfo_low: Redundant — FCF/CFO >= 25% direct gate is stricter than the 15% forensic alert
excluded_flags_rationale.malik_flags: rf_high_cash_debt, rf_itr_declining, rf_ssgr_deficit, rf_low_fcf_ebitda, rf_opm_volatile, rf_nfat_very_low — Vijay Malik framework; not cited in Diamonds in the Dust
excluded_flags_rationale.sector_flags: rf_cwip_bloat, rf_capex_mirage, rf_lease_inflation — Ind AS 116 / IL&FS / infrastructure-specific
excluded_flags_rationale.governance_flags: rf_psu_value_destruction, rf_tax_panic — WCS24 / PSU specific, not Mukherjea's Diamond lens

forensic_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — stocks with null forensic data fail conservatively

lens_2_competitive_moat._purpose: Identifies structural ROCE durability — sustained above-cost returns across a full business cycle.

roce_10y_floor.rationale: Mukherjea Chapter 4: ROCE > 15% sustained for 10 consecutive years proves the competitive moat is structural, not cyclical. The 10Y window spans two full Indian business cycles. This is identical to Coffee Can's ROCE gate but Coffee Can uses a single 10Y average; Diamonds requires year-by-year consistency (book). Proxy: roce_med_10y (10Y median) used as a consistency proxy since year-by-year 10Y series is not in CSV.

roce_5y_floor.rationale: Engineering addition: ROCE median over 5Y must also be >= 15%. This guards against a company with strong 10Y ROCE that is currently in structural decline (recent 5Y below 15% while older years inflate the 10Y median). Together roce_med_10y >= 15 AND roce_med_5y >= 15 approximates the 'stable or rising' trajectory requirement.

lens_3_capital_allocation._purpose: Verifies management reinvests surplus cash into value-creating projects — self-sufficient compounders.

cumulative_fcf_to_ccfo.rationale: Mukherjea Chapter 5: companies that generate FCF >= 25% of their cumulative operating cash flows over 10 years are self-sufficient capital allocators — they fund all organic expansion internally without dilution or debt. 25% floor means at least 1 in 4 rupees of operating cash survives after sustaining growth capex. The CSV lacks 10Y cumulative series; fcf_to_cfo_pct (point-in-time FCF/CFO ratio) is the best proxy available.
cumulative_fcf_to_ccfo.known_limitation: Book requires 10Y cumulative FCF/CFO. CSV only provides point-in-time FCF and OCF — cumulative_fcf_to_ccfo in data_engine.py aliases fcf_to_cfo_pct as best proxy. Single-year FCF/CFO is more volatile than the 10Y cumulative (a company in a high-capex year fails even if cumulative FCF/CFO is strong). Severity: LOW — fcf_to_cfo_pct still identifies capital-intensive businesses correctly in most cases.

stage_1_screen._purpose: Universe reduction before the Three-Lens analysis. Eliminates sub-scale and declining businesses.

revenue_growth_10y.rationale: Stage 1 filter: consistent long-term demand growth is a prerequisite for moat verification. 10% matches Coffee Can's CAGR threshold. Uses rev_gr_10y (10Y revenue CAGR from CSV). fillna(0) fails conservatively — insufficient 10Y history cannot qualify as a Diamond.

revenue_growth_5y_guard.rationale: Recent deceleration guard: a company with strong 10Y revenue but collapsing recent growth (e.g., post-peak cycle) should not pass. 8% is below the 10Y threshold to allow for normal cyclicality while flagging structural deterioration. fillna(0) conservative.

balance_sheet_quality.rationale: Strictest D/E gate across all Mukherjea books (Coffee Can < 1.0, Unusual Billionaires < 1.0, Diamonds < 0.5). A leveraged company services debt first, limiting reinvestment into moat-building activities. Financial sector exempted: banks use leverage structurally; their quality is captured by ROCE/forensic gates.

size_floor.rationale: Mukherjea focuses on businesses with proven institutional visibility and data quality. ₹500 Cr floor eliminates micro-caps with fragile models. This is higher than the 100-Bagger floor (₹200 Cr) because Diamonds requires 10Y ROCE history — micro-caps rarely have this. fillna(0) fails conservatively.

rejected_gates.rejected_reason: RPT data not in StockScan CSV. The forensic_score == 0 gate provides partial coverage through its component flags but cannot verify the specific 5% RPT threshold.
Auditor quality data not in StockScan CSV. No automated proxy available.

rejected_gates.gate: year-by-year CFO/PAT >= 0.8 for each of 10 years
rejected_gates.rejected_reason: Requires 10Y annual CFO/PAT series. CSV only has trailing 12M cfo_to_pat. Point-in-time proxy (>= 80%) used instead. forensic_score == 0 catches severe multi-year earnings manipulation.
Requires multi-year annual depreciation series. Not in CSV. Cannot implement reliably.

known_gaps.gap: cfo_to_pat point-in-time vs year-by-year (10Y required by book)
known_gaps.mitigation: forensic_score == 0 provides multi-year forensic integrity. Point-in-time CFO/PAT >= 80% catches current year quality. Together these approximate the book's annual consistency requirement.

known_gaps.gap: cumulative_fcf_to_ccfo uses point-in-time fcf_to_cfo_pct — no 10Y cumulative CFO/FCF series in CSV
known_gaps.mitigation: Point-in-time FCF/CFO still identifies capital-intensive vs self-sufficient businesses. Companies consistently above 25% FCF/CFO annually will easily pass the cumulative threshold too.

known_gaps.gap: RPT ceiling (5% of revenue) and Big 4 auditor gate not implementable
known_gaps.mitigation: forensic_score == 0 hard gate provides the strictest multi-flag integrity check in the platform as a composite governance proxy.
